scores.one_line_justification: Flush honours quiet hours (leaves batch pending) and throttle exactly as release_deferred; thorough R11 spec text, updated docstrings, shared DELIVERY_MODES constants, and tests pin both flush interactions with exact ids and audit reasons.

send_digests never checks quiet hours, delivering to a user at 23:00 local in breach of R5; the rewritten R11 exempts the flush without reconciling R5 and a test pins the defect, though the throttle-requeue and queued_digest audit choices are otherwise clean and recorded.

Flush re-queues still-quiet batches and mirrors release_deferred's throttle handling as its spec and docstring state; tests cover both interactions (order asserted a little loosely), with minor nits: mode literals duplicated in cli and a stale Delivery kind docstring.

send_digests applies the throttle but skips quiet hours entirely, so a digest lands mid-quiet-hours against R5; R11 was rewritten to bless this without amending R5, and a test pins the wrong behaviour, though breadth elsewhere (unsubscribed-digest, urgent, CLI) is good.

Flush honours quiet hours and keeps throttled batches queued (retried next day, order preserved via front-requeue), with the choice recorded in R11 and tested end-to-end; minor drift only (DELIVERY_MODES lives in routing.py, cli duplicates the literals, stale Delivery docstring).
